specialCases.description: Hypixel is one of the largest Minecraft server networks. It is NOT a modpack or a mod. Players connect to it via the server address play.hypixel.net. Hypixel supports Minecraft 1.8.9 and 1.21.2+ (currently 1.21.11 is the standard). While no mods are REQUIRED, there are many ALLOWED client-side helper mods that improve the experience without triggering the anti-cheat (Watchdog). These mods are visual/informational only — they do not automate gameplay, modify packets, or give unfair advantages.
specialCases.action: Create a Fabric 1.21.11 installation for Hypixel Skyblock. Install client-side helper mods like Skyblocker and SkyHanni from Modrinth. Do NOT install server-side mods or any mod that automates actions (auto-mining, auto-clicking, reach hacks, speed hacks). Do NOT install PackCore — it is only useful as part of the SkyBlock Enhanced modpack. Tell the user to connect to play.hypixel.net from the in-game server list.

specialCases.description: Hypixel Skyblock is a specific game mode ON the Hypixel server (play.hypixel.net). It features custom skills, dungeons, minions, bazaar, auction house — all server-side. While no mods are REQUIRED to play, there is a large ecosystem of ALLOWED client-side helper mods that add HUD overlays, puzzle solvers, tooltips, and QoL features. These mods are widely used and considered safe by the community. Key mods: Skyblocker (dungeon solvers, HUDs, tooltips), SkyHanni (garden overlays, Diana helpers, slayer helpers), Odin (endgame dungeons/Kuudra), ScamScreener (safety). The current standard version is Fabric 1.21.11. The SkyBlock Enhanced modpack on Modrinth bundles 60+ pre-configured mods.
specialCases.action: Create a Fabric 1.21.11 installation. Install Skyblocker and SkyHanni as the core helper mods (search 'skyblocker' and 'skyhanni' on Modrinth, loader=fabric). Add performance mods (Sodium, Lithium, FerriteCore) and Fabric API. For a full setup, also add Odin, ScamScreener, Bazaar Utils, and SkyBlock Profile Viewer. Do NOT install PackCore unless the user is using the SkyBlock Enhanced modpack — it is a companion mod that only works within that modpack and does nothing on its own. Tell the user to connect to play.hypixel.net and select Skyblock from the lobby menu. NEVER install mods that automate gameplay (auto-mining, auto-combat, reach hacks, movement speed) — these WILL get the user banned by Watchdog.
specialCases.commonConfusion: Users often confuse Hypixel Skyblock with the classic Skyblock map or modded Skyblock. If someone says 'I want to play Skyblock', always ask: 'Do you mean Hypixel Skyblock (the online server game mode), or a singleplayer Skyblock world on your own?'
specialCases.bannedMods: Any mod that automates actions (auto-clickers, auto-miners, auto-combat), modifies reach distance, increases movement speed, or sends automated packets. Examples of BANNED mods: Baritone, X-Ray mods, Kill Aura mods, auto-farm bots. These result in permanent Watchdog bans.
specialCases.safeMods: All client-side informational/visual mods are safe: Skyblocker, SkyHanni, Odin, Firmament, Sodium, Lithium, FerriteCore, AppleSkin, Xaero's Minimap, REI/JEI, chat filters, HUD overlays, resource packs. These do not modify server packets or automate gameplay.

specialCases.description: Skyblock can mean THREE very different things: (1) Hypixel Skyblock — a popular game mode on the Hypixel multiplayer server with many ALLOWED client-side helper mods (Skyblocker, SkyHanni, etc.), Fabric 1.21.11 is the standard. (2) Classic Skyblock map — a singleplayer survival challenge where you spawn on a tiny floating island with limited resources, played on a Vanilla world. (3) Modded Skyblock — mods like 'Sky Factory', 'Stoneblock', 'All The Mods' that add Skyblock-style progression to a local world.
specialCases.action: Always ask the user to clarify: 'Do you want to play Hypixel Skyblock (online server with helper mods), or create a local singleplayer Skyblock world?' Based on the answer: (1) Hypixel Skyblock = Fabric 1.21.11 install + Skyblocker + SkyHanni + performance mods + join play.hypixel.net. (2) Classic = Vanilla install + create a Skyblock map world. (3) Modded = install the specific modpack via Modrinth.
specialCases.quickDecision: If the user says 'Hypixel Skyblock' or 'sb' or 'hypixel sb' → Fabric 1.21.11 + Skyblocker + SkyHanni + join play.hypixel.net. If they say 'skyblock mod' or 'skyblock modpack' → search Modrinth. If unclear → ask.

specialCases.description: OptiFine is a popular graphics/shader mod but it is NOT available on Modrinth or CurseForge. It must be downloaded manually from optifine.net. For Fabric users, Sodium + Iris Shaders is the recommended modern alternative that performs better.
specialCases.action: If the user wants OptiFine on Fabric, recommend installing Sodium + Iris Shaders instead (search 'Sodium' and 'Iris' on Modrinth). If they specifically want OptiFine, inform them it must be downloaded from https://optifine.net and placed manually in the mods folder.

specialCases.description: Minecraft Bedrock Edition (Windows Store, console, mobile) is a completely different product from Java Edition. This launcher only supports Java Edition.
specialCases.action: Inform the user that Nexora Launcher only supports Java Edition. If they want Bedrock, they need the official Minecraft Launcher from the Microsoft Store.

specialCases.description: Lunar Client is a third-party Minecraft client for PvP on older versions (mainly 1.8.9). It is not a modloader and cannot be installed through this launcher.
specialCases.action: Inform the user that Lunar Client is a separate launcher and cannot be managed here. Suggest creating a Vanilla 1.8.9 install with PvP mods from Modrinth as an alternative.

specialCases.description: When a user wants to JOIN an existing multiplayer server (not host one themselves), they need a Vanilla or appropriate client installation and they add the server address in the in-game multiplayer screen.
specialCases.action: Create a Vanilla installation (or the appropriate modded one if the server requires it) and instruct the user to launch the game, click Multiplayer, click Add Server, and paste the server IP.

specialCases.description: CurseForge is a mod hosting platform. This launcher now supports BOTH Modrinth AND CurseForge for mod discovery. When searching for a mod, the AI should try Modrinth first (larger free API, no key needed), and fall back to CurseForge if the mod is not found. Some mods are exclusive to CurseForge.
specialCases.action: When a user asks for a mod: search Modrinth first. If not found, search CurseForge. If still not found, tell the user the mod was not found on either platform and suggest manual download. Always tell the user which platform the mod was found on.

specialCases.description: Forge and NeoForge are two separate mod loaders and are treated as separate filters on Modrinth. A mod listed only under 'forge' on Modrinth will NOT be found when searching with loader=neoforge, and vice versa. They are NOT interchangeable for mod searches. NeoForge is the modern fork for 1.20.1+ but many mods are specifically published as 'forge' only.
specialCases.action: Always use the exact loader the user specified. If a mod is not found for loader X, do NOT silently switch to the other loader — instead report that the mod was not found for that loader and ask the user if they want to try the other one.

specialCases.description: Fabric is a lightweight mod loader popular for newer Minecraft versions. Fabric mods are NOT compatible with Forge/NeoForge and vice versa. Fabric has its own mod ecosystem on Modrinth.
specialCases.action: When installing mods for Fabric, always filter by loader=fabric. If a mod is not available for Fabric, suggest the user try Forge/NeoForge or look for a Fabric-equivalent mod (e.g. Sodium instead of OptiFine).

specialCases.description: Enhanced vanilla is a playstyle where the user wants to keep Minecraft feeling like vanilla but with quality-of-life improvements, technical tweaks, and subtle additions. It does NOT mean big content mods like Create or tech mods. Instead it means: Carpet (technical server tweaks), Lithium (performance), AppleSkin (hunger overlay), Xaero's Minimap, JEI/REI (recipe viewer), WTHIT/Jade (block info overlay), Roughly Enough Resources, Mouse Tweaks, Inventory Profiles.
specialCases.action: For enhanced vanilla, recommend these mods in order of importance: (1) Sodium + Lithium + FerriteCore (performance), (2) AppleSkin (food info), (3) Xaero's Minimap + World Map (navigation), (4) REI or JEI (recipe viewer), (5) Jade/WTHIT (block info overlay), (6) Mouse Tweaks (inventory QoL), (7) BetterF3 (debug screen cleanup). Explain that these keep the vanilla feel while making the game smoother and more informative.
specialCases.serverSideNote: For servers, Carpet is the gold standard technical mod — it adds configurable vanilla-friendly tweaks like faster leaf decay, stackable shulker boxes, and adjustable tick speed. Search 'Carpet' on Modrinth.

specialCases.description: Carpet is a technical Minecraft mod for servers (mainly Fabric) that adds configurable vanilla-friendly tweaks without changing core gameplay. Features include: stackable shulker boxes, adjustable tick speed, faster leaf decay, xp bottle fixing, renewable resources tweaks, mob farm optimizations. It is widely used on technical Minecraft servers. There are extensions like 'Carpet Extra' and 'Carpet TIS Addition' that add more features.
specialCases.action: When the user wants Carpet, install it for their Fabric server or installation. Search 'carpet' on Modrinth with loader=fabric. If they want more features, also suggest 'Carpet Extra'. Carpet is server-side, so it can be installed on just the server — clients do not need it (but can install it for extra client features).

specialCases.description: Technical Minecraft refers to advanced gameplay focused on game mechanics exploitation: redstone contraptions, mob farms, item sorting systems, TNT duplication, chunk loading, zero-tick farms. Popular mods: Carpet (server tweaks), Lithium (optimizations that don't change mechanics), and sometimes specific farm-related mods.
specialCases.action: For technical Minecraft players, recommend: (1) Carpet + Carpet Extra (configurable vanilla tweaks), (2) Lithium (performance without changing game mechanics — important: unlike Sodium, Lithium does not change how redstone or farms work), (3) a high-performance setup with Sodium + FerriteCore. Warn that some optimization mods (like Starlight) can subtly change lighting mechanics which may affect certain technical builds.

specialCases.description: Horror mods add fear, suspense, and dark atmosphere to Minecraft. Popular horror mods: Man from the Fog (terrifying stalker entity in fog), The Man from the Window (scary entity that watches you), Cave Dweller (creature that lives in caves), From the Fog (Herobrine-like stalker), Herobrine mod, The Backrooms (liminal space dimension). These are standalone mods, not modpacks.
specialCases.action: When users ask for horror mods, search Modrinth for 'horror' or 'scary' mods. Recommend starting with 'Man from the Fog' as it is the most popular. For full horror atmosphere, also suggest: (1) Ambient Sounds or Sound Physics for immersive audio, (2) Dynamic Darkness or True Darkness for scary lighting, (3) a shader pack like BSL or Complementary with fog effects. Warn that horror mods are best played in singleplayer or small private servers.

specialCases.description: Shaders are visual enhancement mods that change lighting, shadows, water, and sky rendering. On Fabric: install Iris Shaders (shader loader) + Sodium (performance). On Forge/NeoForge: install Oculus (shader loader) + Embeddium (performance). Popular shader packs: Complementary Shaders (balanced), BSL Shaders (cinematic), SEUS (ultra-realistic), Sildur's Enhanced Default (lightweight), Rethinking Voxels (ray tracing-like).
specialCases.action: For shaders: (1) Fabric users: install Iris + Sodium from Modrinth. (2) Forge users: install Oculus + Embeddium. (3) Then the user needs to download a shader pack .zip and place it in the shaderpacks folder. Recommend Complementary Shaders as the best balanced option for most PCs. For low-end PCs, suggest Sildur's Enhanced Default.
specialCases.note: Shader packs themselves cannot be installed via Modrinth — they are separate .zip files the user downloads and places in the shaderpacks folder.

specialCases.description: Modpacks are curated collections of mods packaged together. Popular modpacks: All The Mods (ATM) series, FTB (Feed The Beast) packs, RLCraft (hardcore survival), Better MC (enhanced vanilla), Vault Hunters, Create: Above and Beyond, Pixelmon. Many modpacks are available as downloads from Modrinth or CurseForge.
specialCases.action: When a user wants a modpack: (1) Search Modrinth first for the modpack name. (2) If not found, try CurseForge. (3) If found, install it using the modpack import/install command. (4) If the modpack is not on either platform, guide the user to download the .mrpack or .zip file and import it manually.

specialCases.description: RLCraft is a very popular hardcore survival modpack known for extreme difficulty. It includes mods like First Aid (locational damage), Tough as Nails (thirst/temperature), Recurrent Complex (dungeons), and many combat/difficulty mods. It is primarily a Forge modpack for Minecraft 1.12.2.
specialCases.action: RLCraft is a CurseForge modpack. Search CurseForge for 'RLCraft'. It requires Forge 1.12.2. Warn the user that it is extremely difficult and designed to kill you constantly.

specialCases.description: Quality of life (QoL) mods improve the Minecraft experience without changing core gameplay. Essential QoL mods: (1) JEI or REI — shows all crafting recipes in a searchable menu, (2) Jade/WTHIT/HWYLA — shows what block/entity you are looking at, (3) Xaero's Minimap — small map in the corner, (4) AppleSkin — shows saturation/hunger info on food, (5) Mouse Tweaks — scroll wheel inventory management, (6) Controlling — rebind any key, (7) BetterF3 — cleaner F3 debug screen, (8) Inventory Profiles Next — auto-sort inventory.
specialCases.action: For QoL mods, install them one by one. Start with REI (recipe viewer), Jade (block info), Xaero's Minimap, and AppleSkin. These are all on Modrinth and work on both Fabric and Forge.

specialCases.description: World generation mods change how Minecraft generates terrain. Popular ones: Terralith (massive biome overhaul, no new blocks), Tectonic (smoother, more realistic mountains), Terralith + Tectonic together, William Wythers' Overhauled Overworld, Nullscape (better Nether), BetterEnd (better End dimension). Data-pack-based generators like Terralith work on vanilla servers.
specialCases.action: Terralith is the most popular and works on both Fabric and Forge. It is a data pack that replaces world generation without adding new blocks, so it works on vanilla servers too. Search 'Terralith' on Modrinth. For a complete overhaul, combine Terralith (overworld) + Nullscape (nether) + BetterEnd (end).

specialCases.description: When a mod search returns no results, it could be because: (1) The mod name is misspelled, (2) The mod is not available for the selected Minecraft version, (3) The mod is not available for the selected loader (Fabric vs Forge), (4) The mod only exists on one platform (Modrinth or CurseForge but not both).
specialCases.action: Before giving up: (1) Try a shorter/simpler search term (e.g. 'create' instead of 'create mod'), (2) Try the other platform (CurseForge if Modrinth failed), (3) Try without the loader filter to see all versions. If still not found, explain clearly to the user why it was not found and suggest alternatives.

specialCases.description: Server crashes are usually caused by: (1) Incompatible mods (wrong Minecraft version or loader), (2) Missing dependencies (a mod requires another mod), (3) Not enough RAM allocated, (4) Corrupt world or config files.
specialCases.action: First read the server console output to find the error. Look for 'Caused by' lines. If it mentions a specific mod, try removing it. If it mentions a missing dependency, install that dependency. Check that all mods match the server's Minecraft version and loader.

specialCases.description: Common performance mods for Fabric: Sodium (rendering), Lithium (server-side), Starlight (lighting engine), FerriteCore (memory), EntityCulling (skip rendering unseen entities), ModernFix (startup + misc), ImmediatelyFast (HUD rendering). For Forge/NeoForge: Embeddium (Sodium port), Canary (Lithium port), FerriteCore, ModernFix.
specialCases.action: When asked about performance, recommend the appropriate optimization mods for the user's loader. Sodium is the #1 most impactful performance mod for Fabric. For a complete setup, recommend: Sodium + Lithium + FerriteCore + EntityCulling + ModernFix. For very low-end PCs, also reduce render distance and entity distance in video settings.
specialCases.importantNote: Starlight changes the lighting engine and may break technical redstone builds. If the user plays technical Minecraft, do NOT recommend Starlight — use Lithium instead.

specialCases.description: Skyblocker is the most comprehensive Hypixel Skyblock helper mod. Fabric-only, supports 1.21.11 (latest releases like v6.x). Client-side only, 3.6M+ downloads on Modrinth. Features include: Dungeon puzzle solvers (all F1-F7 puzzles, terminal solvers), secret waypoints, minimap & score, Croesus profit calculator, party finder GUI, mining waypoints (Dwarven Mines, Crystal Hollows, Glacite Tunnels), foraging helpers, Kuudra waypoints, Crimson Isles dojo helper, slayer helpers (Vampire, Enderman, Blaze), garden farming HUD (crops/min, coins/h, visitor helper), Rift features, Spider's Den hidden relics, health/mana/defense/XP bars, Bazaar/AH price tooltips, profile viewer, fancy tab HUD, item protection, wiki lookup (F4), quick navigation, recipe book, backpack preview, chat filters, Discord Rich Presence. Modrinth ID: y6DuFGwJ.
specialCases.action: Search 'skyblocker' on Modrinth with loader=fabric. Install for any Hypixel Skyblock setup. This is the #1 essential Hypixel Skyblock mod. Pair with SkyHanni for maximum coverage.

specialCases.description: SkyHanni is a major Hypixel Skyblock helper mod focused on Garden, Slayer, Fishing, Bingo, Diana, Rift, and Mining features. Available for both Fabric and Forge. Supports 1.8.9, 1.21.10-1.21.11, and newer. Client-side only, 3.45M+ downloads on Modrinth. Key features: Garden crop milestone display with money-per-hour calculation, visitor helper with bazaar prices, Diana waypoint estimates + Inquisitor sharing, Mythological Ritual helper, damage indicators, Slayer helpers (all types), fishing helpers, Bingo guide, customizable HUDs, cleaner scoreboard, chat filters. Modrinth ID: byNkmv5G.
specialCases.action: Search 'skyhanni' on Modrinth. Install for Hypixel Skyblock alongside Skyblocker. SkyHanni excels at Garden/farming/Diana features while Skyblocker excels at dungeons/mining — they complement each other well. Both are safe and allowed on Hypixel.

specialCases.description: Odin is a Hypixel Skyblock helper mod focused on endgame PvE content: dungeons (especially Floor 7) and Kuudra. Features include: puzzle solvers for most F7 terminals and dungeon puzzles, Wither Dragon tracking, Melody helpers, full run statistics, Kuudra pearl waypoints and supply tracking. Client-side, Fabric, safe for Hypixel.
specialCases.action: Search 'odin' on Modrinth with loader=fabric. Install for endgame dungeon/Kuudra players. Pairs well with Skyblocker (which has broader coverage but less F7 specialization).

specialCases.description: SkyBlock Enhanced is a comprehensive Hypixel Skyblock modpack on Modrinth with 60+ pre-configured mods for Fabric 1.21.11+. It includes Skyblocker, SkyHanni, Odin, ScamScreener, Firmament, all performance mods (Sodium, Lithium, FerriteCore, EntityCulling), UI QoL mods, shader support (Iris + Complementary), and a guided first-launch setup wizard with resolution presets (1080p, 1440p, 4K). 1.25M+ downloads. Modrinth ID: e0oMrxjp. Includes PackCore (its own companion mod for setup wizard and custom start menu).
specialCases.action: If the user wants a complete Hypixel Skyblock setup without choosing individual mods, recommend the SkyBlock Enhanced modpack. Search 'SkyBlock Enhanced' on Modrinth. It handles everything: mod selection, configuration, HUD positioning, and backup management.

specialCases.description: PackCore is a companion mod EXCLUSIVELY for the SkyBlock Enhanced modpack. It provides the custom Hypixel Skyblock-themed start menu, setup wizard, and configuration management. It does NOTHING useful when installed standalone — it has no gameplay features, no HUDs, no solvers. It is NOT a helper mod for Hypixel Skyblock.
specialCases.action: NEVER install PackCore as a standalone mod. It only works as part of the SkyBlock Enhanced modpack. If the user asks for Hypixel Skyblock helper mods, do NOT include PackCore — recommend Skyblocker and SkyHanni instead.

specialCases.description: Complete list of allowed Hypixel Skyblock helper mods available on Modrinth (all Fabric, client-side, safe for Watchdog): Core helpers: Skyblocker (dungeon/mining/UI), SkyHanni (garden/Diana/slayer), Odin (F7/Kuudra), Firmament (utility), ScamScreener (safety). Trading: Bazaar Utils, MarketGuard. Storage: Skyblock Enhanced Storage, SkyOcean (searchable storage + item values). UI: SkyBlock Profile Viewer, Modern Warp Menu, Scale Me (1.8 sword block + model scaling), Enhanced Chat. Performance: Sodium, Lithium, FerriteCore, EntityCulling, ModernFix, ImmediatelyFast, Krypton, BadOptimizations, More Culling. General QoL: AppleSkin, Xaero's Minimap, REI/JEI, Zoomify, 3D Skin Layers, Controlling, BetterF3, No Chat Reports. DO NOT install: PackCore (only works inside SkyBlock Enhanced modpack, useless standalone), Caxton, Language Reload, Reliable Recipe Viewer (modpack-specific utilities). All these mods are client-side and do NOT automate gameplay.
specialCases.action: For a basic Hypixel Skyblock setup: Fabric 1.21.11 + Fabric API + Skyblocker + SkyHanni + Sodium + Lithium + FerriteCore. For a full setup, add: Odin, ScamScreener, Bazaar Utils, EntityCulling, ModernFix, AppleSkin, Xaero's Minimap. Do NOT install PackCore, Caxton, or Language Reload — these are modpack-specific and do nothing useful on their own. For the complete pre-configured experience, recommend the SkyBlock Enhanced modpack instead.
